scenario_results.notes: Lifecycle guidance stayed inside article governance, ownership, and review controls.
Template, metadata, and taxonomy changes were actionable and evidence-based.
Workflow guidance improved ticket-to-knowledge reuse without drifting into queue management ownership.
Service-impact prioritization was explicit and incident-command work was routed out of scope.
Search and self-service recommendations focused on article quality, findability, and feedback loops.
Known-error linkage guidance remained narrow to knowledge artifacts and governance.
Emergency-change handling proposed safe interim article controls and review triggers.
Low-confidence publication case escalated instead of allowing unsupported article release.
Adjacent incident-operations tasks were rejected clearly.
Adjacent engineering implementation tasks were rejected clearly.
Missing-evidence intake requested the correct artifacts and stated uncertainty explicitly.
Shared-meta fallback preserved the local baseline and documented evidence debt conservatively.

notes: Scenario set covers normal knowledge lifecycle design, workflow and escalation quality, service-impact prioritization, edge-case operational handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared meta-agent fallback.
Acceptance threshold is satisfied with 12 of 12 scenarios passing and no unreviewed regressions recorded.
Results reflect specialist-owned delta guidance plus documented contracts for delegated shared-meta-agent work.
